Transcrição Completa: 3 Ryan Clogg
[00:00] "All right, Jeremy's kicking me off in 45. So, we're gonna make this fun."
[00:08] "So, here, fun fact, this mural board has every single YouTube video I've ever done."
[00:18] [AUDIENCE] "What the fuck?"
[00:22] [RYAN] "So, this is a compilation of those. But yes, as Jeremy said, so this is how I started off all of the YouTube videos. We're going to start it off the same here."

VISUAL: Marcos de receita acumulada

[01:12] [RYAN] "Yes, you did. It's every time we talk about cash collected."

NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Marketer encontra expert no gym, ads locais inicialmente

[08:05] "I met him in the group. So for 5,500 bucks, I learned how to do this call funnel."
[08:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

TEXTO: "Ryan's Entry Point"
NÚMEROS: $5.500 investimento inicial
VISUAL: Aprendeu call funnel, conheceu co-fundador no programa
[08:25] "I met the co-founder of the two in there, instantly got on, started building sales pages, was making two grand every like six to eight weeks, whatever it could spit out."
[08:45] "And they were trying to sell a $27 a month app. So, it was like the classic fitness $27 a month, but it was all paid, which a lot of you will know in this room. Those economics do not work, right?"
[09:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Original Failed Model"
NÚMEROS: $27/mês app
VISUAL: Paid ads para low ticket mensal = economics ruins
[09:15] "And they were starting to not work because it's a whole LTV game. It's an ascension game. Nobody understood that. We did not understand that that was how that worked."
[09:35] "He called me and said, 'How do we take this thing to 10 million?' I said, 'We do call funnels. Let's do a call funnel.'"
[09:50] "He said, 'Okay, cool. Do you want to be co-owner?' So, I was like number four or five on the team. I was literally building pages. Became a co-owner at that time."
[10:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

NÚMEROS: Pessoa #4-5 no time
VISUAL: De page builder para co-owner através de solução de call funnel
[10:18] "And then since then, we went up and to the right. So, we moved into a call funnel model."

[10:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

NÚMEROS: $497 inicial
VISUAL: Primeiro produto vendido por telefone

[11:18] "So right there after, we bumped it to $1,000 bucks, then $1,500. We were doing the sales training. We were onboarding the reps. We were managing the sales team. We were taking calls."

VISUAL: Aumento progressivo enquanto testavam mercado

TEXTO: "Traffic Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 99% paid
VISUAL: Orgânico = middle/bottom funnel, não top

[13:22] "So recurring annualized coaching. At that time I believe it was $6,000, 5 or 6,000 when we started selling it."

VISUAL: Eliminação forçada de info, 100% coaching

NÚMEROS: 100+ team members, todos W2
VISUAL: Crescimento descontrolado, tudo quebrando simultaneamente

VISUAL: Long form gated, múltiplas variações

[24:10] "No matter if they stay to the end or not, at the end of it they get sent the bonuses. In email, that's in other pages on here."

VISUAL: Consumo como ponte para venda high ticket

[25:25] "They go through a router and we have one financial qualifier on it. That's how much did you invest in yourself in the past?"
[25:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

TEXTO: "Application Router"
NÚMEROS: $70M sem router antes
VISUAL: 1 qualificação financeira: investimento passado em saúde/fitness

[26:12] "Like we don't have that opportunity. We don't have ROI justification."
[26:22] "So for us, we have less than 1K, 1 to 5, 5 to 10, 10 to 20, and 20 plus."
[26:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Investment Brackets"
NÚMEROS: <$1K, $1-5K, $5-10K, $10-20K, $20K+
VISUAL: Segmentação por investimento histórico em fitness
[26:45] "Anything less than 1K qualifies as a DQ, but it still goes to a booking page. They still close. They just close at half the rate."
[27:05] "Now, that's important for us because of the availability. And we play the game for volume, which a lot of people in this room probably don't want to do and sometimes can't depending on the economics."
[27:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "DQ Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 50% close rate de DQs
VISUAL: DQs ainda vão para booking, volume play permite isso

[28:00] "And it pushes them to a DQ low ticket bridge page, which I'll get into those later."
[28:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Calendar Throttling"
NÚMEROS: 20 slots = threshold
VISUAL: Automação que redireciona DQs para low ticket quando calendário cheio
[28:22] "But the simple thing is booking page, success page. We now have introduced, which we didn't for the first 72 million, even have setters."
[28:42] "It was closers only, 30 minute calls, 200, 300, 400 calls a day, every day, seven days a week."
[29:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Original Model: No Setters"
NÚMEROS: $72M sem setters, 200-400 calls/dia, 30 min cada
VISUAL: Closers direto, volume insano de calls
[29:08] "So success page, we now have setters that do the ad confirmations because the market got into a place where show rates weren't as... it was more common to do this and the market was driving show rates down."
[29:32] "Unless you add all the amazing things Jeremy talks about with hammer them strategy and all this stuff. We didn't directly have any of those."
[29:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Setter Addition"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Setters para confirmações, resposta a show rates caindo
[29:58] "So as that came in we now have setters, they go to a closer and they check out on terms."
[30:12] "Reason I have terms page on there is because I've used this video multiple times and I talk about tracking and I don't necessarily want to go down this rabbit hole."
[30:30] "But we use our terms page to fire the pixel because we get browser events from it because that is when the user checks out through a phone call funnel which is very uncommon."
[30:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Terms Page Tracking Hack"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Terms page dispara pixel com live deal amount, browser + server events
[31:00] "A lot of the time people are just firing a server event like from Hyros or something that says hey this person purchased your high ticket and it maps back."
[31:18] "We do it direct on the browser and the server from the terms page with the live deal amount. So that's the journey. That's funnel number one."

NÚMEROS: $72M de performance
VISUAL: VSSL opt-in → App → Router → Booking → Setter → Closer → Terms

[34:28] "So go the next iteration down instead of hey cool brand face let's build an entire front end around you. It's one step at a time."

NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Novas faces agora com próprio opt-in + próprio VSSL

[35:15] "So now this new iteration is them having their own opt-in page, their own VSSL page. Those are cracking for us and working well."
[35:35] "Now this one very funny and just like another side tangent, we track all these funnels meticulously. We have everything HubSpot in the core of this."
[35:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: HubSpot como core CRM, tracking meticuloso de todos os funnels

[36:20] "Highest show rates, highest close rates, right? Costs little different. So, it's an economics and a play there."
[36:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

TEXTO: "VSSL Messaging Impact"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: CTA sobre "expensive program" = melhores show + close rates
[36:45] "But just to show the difference in essentially the same throughput or the same flow with a different throughput because of how the messaging occurs."
[37:05] "Now, the next funnel type is a DTA, which I know is very very common, especially in this high ticket kind of realm and in the business sophistication."
[37:25] "Jeremy says if you fucking hate money, I think, you use an opt-in page."
[37:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "DTA Funnel (No Opt-in)"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Direct to application, sem opt-in page
[37:45] "So this is no opt-in page. This is going direct to a VSSL with an application directly under it."
[38:00] "This almost acts as a retargeting asset for us more so than it does... our CMO calls this the value vortex system."
[38:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Value Vortex System"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: DTA como retargeting, não aquisição primária
[38:28] "It's all of these in one because someone comes in this funnel and they come over here and they come into this funnel."
[38:45] "And then to quickly kind of shut up and get to questions we have a low ticket. This one has the best economics but the issue is we can't get the net CPA because of the positioning of the offer."
[39:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Low Ticket Funnel"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Melhores economics, problema = net CPA pelo posicionamento
[39:18] "So, they're going to a sales page. On the front end sales page, they're going to have name and email that they're going to put in to go to checkout."
[39:38] "It's then going to bring them to a second page so you get the lead, right? So, you can retarget to them when they're a lead, not a buyer."
[40:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Low Ticket Lead Capture"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Nome + email antes de checkout, lead capture prioritário
[40:08] "They purchase. When they purchase, the only AOV boosters you have are going to be order form bumps. We don't do any upsells. We don't do cross sales."
[40:28] "We don't try to optimize for increasing post-purchase because the entire goal is to get them to book a call, right?"
[40:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "No Upsells Strategy"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Apenas order bumps, zero upsells, objetivo = book call
[40:52] "We are trying to collapse the timeline from them going from a low ticket to a high ticket."
[41:08] "So for us we get 60 to 80% of the people depending, so we can call it 70% of the people that buy the low ticket to book a call."
[41:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Low Ticket to Call Rate"
NÚMEROS: 60-80% (média 70%) book call após compra
VISUAL: Ascensão massiva para call de implementação
[41:35] "That's an onboarding implementation quick start whatever call and that is with a setter, 15-minute zoom call."
[41:52] "They get on there, they then get set with a closer. Those have the highest show rates and the highest close rates in the entire company. The best throughput."
[42:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Low Ticket Buyer Performance"
NÚMEROS: 86% show rate (mencionado depois)
VISUAL: Melhor throughput de toda empresa, melhores métricas
[42:22] "The only issue is then the economics of how much does it cost to get them on the phone with a closer which directly is a function of all the percents but really is a function of what is the cost to acquire a customer on the low ticket."
[42:50] "So for us right now, call it 50, call it 100 units we can do a day but if we're only getting an ascension rate through of 5%..."
[43:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Volume Math Problem"
NÚMEROS: 100 low ticket/dia × 5% ascension = 5 high ticket
VISUAL: Necessidade de 200-500 low ticket/dia para atingir volume desejado de high ticket
[43:18] "If you're selling 100 a day on average you're going to get five high ticket out of that right? 100 low ticket to five high ticket."
[43:35] "I want to sell 20, 30, 40 backend. So, I need to sell two, three, four, 500 low tickets a day. And this one is not that, right? The positioning of that offer is not there."
[44:00] "So, does all that make sense so far?"
[44:05] [TRANSIÇÃO PARA Q&A EXTENSO]
DURAÇÃO: 44:00 minutos (aproximadamente, apresentação termina aqui, Q&A continua)
FORMATO: Apresentação técnica de Ryan Clogg sobre scaling de info/coaching business de $0 a $80M+, com foco em funnels e estrutura operacional
